task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Enterprise web interface (dashboard or home screen) is visible, indicating a successful login as the 'admin' user.
The Settings > Event Types page or a success message showing the creation of an event type named 'ssh_failed_login' with the search string `index=security_logs "Failed password"`.

task_completion.description: Assign tags to the 'ssh_failed_login' Event Type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Settings > Tags page, Event Type details, or a search view showing the tags 'authentication' and 'failure' associated with the 'ssh_failed_login' event type.
The Settings > Event Types page or a success message showing the creation of an event type named 'apache_critical_error' with the search string `index=web_logs (crit OR emerg OR error)`.

task_completion.description: Assign tags to the 'apache_critical_error' Event Type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Settings > Tags page, Event Type details, or a search view showing the tags 'error' and 'critical' associated with the 'apache_critical_error' event type.

task_completion.description: Create an hourly saved search named 'Unified_SOC_Alerts' using tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Reports, Alerts, or Searches page showing a saved search named 'Unified_SOC_Alerts' with an hourly schedule and a search query that uses the tags (e.g., `tag=failure OR tag=critical`).

integrity.description: The agent used the Splunk Web GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Splunk web interface in a browser, rather than terminal windows executing CLI commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Splunk application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows localhost:8000 (or the appropriate Splunk URL) and standard Splunk UI elements are properly rendered, not a text editor or mock HTML file.

integrity.description: The saved search relies on tags rather than querying the indexes directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: The search string for 'Unified_SOC_Alerts' uses `tag=` syntax rather than explicitly querying `index=security_logs` or `index=web_logs`.
